B-104 architectural principle: Pi = telemetry emitter, server = analytics authority. Pi may compute in-drive aggregates locally (engine on = AC power, no battery cost) but does NOT transmit derived analytics. Pi DOES log drive boundary events (drive_start / drive_end timestamps) for Pi-health/diagnostics -- these are event records, not derived analytics. Server is sole writer of persisted analytics tables.

stories.title: B-104 Step 1a: server-side drive_summary compute from raw (US-326+US-348 redo)
stories.intent: B-104 architectural shift Step 1a. Server reads raw realtime_data + Pi-synced event logs (drive_start_timestamp + ambient_temp_at_start_c + starting_battery_v + data_source) + computes drive_summary fields (start_time, end_time, duration_seconds, row_count, is_real, analytics) on a server-side trigger that does NOT depend on a Pi-side drive-end signal. Pi-side drive_summary computed-field writer retired; Pi keeps event-log fields for diagnostics per Spool data v2 contract. Argus's root cause (DriveDetector drive-end signal does not fire on sequencer-driven termination) becomes structurally moot -- server reads timestamps from realtime_data MIN/MAX, doesn't depend on a marker event. Trigger options for Atlas at dispatch: (a) on every sync batch receipt for any drive_id touched, (b) on detection of a drive's last realtime_data row being older than N minutes (soak window), (c) overnight batch + on-demand. CIO ratified overnight batch + on-demand. Diagnostic-first: pre-flight rg sweep of existing US-326/US-348 server writer surface; document what is being retired vs. preserved.

acceptance: Pre-flight: rg 'auto_analysis|_tryAutoAnalysisTrigger|connection_log.*drive' src/server/ produces an inventory of every live touchpoint of the retired seam; result documented in completionNotes (per Atlas 2026-05-21 17:13 pre-flight pattern adoption).

Server has callable compute path compute_drive_summary(drive_id) in src/server/analytics/drive_summary_compute.py. Reads realtime_data rows for drive_id; computes start_time = MIN(timestamp_utc), end_time = MAX(timestamp_utc), duration_seconds = end_time - start_time, row_count = COUNT(*); enriches with Pi event-log fields (ambient_temp_at_start_c, starting_battery_v, data_source) where available; derives is_real from data_source ('real' -> 1, 'simulator' -> 0, NULL -> NULL not silently 0); UPSERTs idempotently into drive_summary.

On-demand CLI: python -m server.cli.recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id N invokes compute for a single drive; --drive-id-range 11-20 for range; --all-stale for all drives with NULL fields.
Idempotent: compute_drive_summary(N) invoked twice produces identical drive_summary row values (computed_at may update; data values identical).
Gap-detection tripwire: for each drive_id, realtime_data rows share contiguous timestamps; gaps >5 min logged at WARN-level (defensive, not failing the compute).

Outlier methodology pin: compute_drive_statistics(drive_id) MUST compute outlier bounds by reusing src/server/analytics/helpers.computeBasicStats (project convention since V0.27.6 US-324: 2-sigma method, outlier_min = avg - 2.0 * std_dev, outlier_max = avg + 2.0 * std_dev). Do NOT introduce a new outlier methodology (IQR, 3-sigma, z-score, robust statistics, or any other formulation). The downstream ComparisonStatus classifier depends on sigma semantics at 2-sigma/3-sigma thresholds; methodology drift would invalidate every historical drive_statistics row produced by the backfill (US-352) and break the classifier across all subsequent analytics. Spool owns analytics methodology authority per the SSOT pattern applied to derived semantics -- server is the writer, Spool is the methodology authority. stories.title: Side fix: US-345 maxTrailBytes guard fix
stories.intent: Per Argus's drill 2026-05-21: first post-deploy reboot tripped maxTrailBytes=65536 guard on boot-progress trail. Trail accumulated unbounded while F-8 was broken (every shutdown classified crashed_during_operation -> trail never trimmed at CLEAN_COMPLETE). F-8 design SOUND post-US-345 fix; once trail trims on clean shutdowns the unbounded growth stops. Guard interaction is the issue: a one-time post-deploy trail-size cleanup OR a more permissive maxTrailBytes value OR a guard that auto-trims rather than refusing-to-write. Atlas + Ralph finalize the fix shape at dispatch. Argus's filed finding at tester/findings/2026-05-21-us-345-f8-fix-blocked-by-maxtrailbytes-guard.md has the technical detail (Argus-lane file; PM does not read directly per lane discipline; Atlas mediates if cross-lane reference needed). Non-chain-blocking; rides V0.27.17 alongside the B-104 work.

acceptance: Fix shape: auto-trim with WARN log emission on write (Atlas-preferred over refuse-to-write or unconditional cap-raise). Don't refuse the write (Argus's V0.27.16 drill: refuse-to-write blocks boot progress logging entirely -> observability hole). Don't silently truncate without logging (silent failure mode). Ralph may challenge with empirically simpler shape via flag-don't-improvise.

stories.title: Side fix: deploy-pi.sh daemon-reload + service restart gap
stories.intent: Per Argus's morning find 2026-05-21: V0.27.16 deploy wrote files to disk + bumped .deploy-version but eclipse-powerwatch.service did NOT restart + daemon-reload did NOT run. Pi was running V0.27.15 code in memory despite .deploy-version reporting V0.27.16/5837239. Argus worked around with bench daemon-reload + reboot before drill. Would have produced false-negative F-7/F-8 validation if not caught. Per Atlas 2026-05-21 accuracy review: deploy-pi.sh ALREADY HAS daemon-reload + restart logic (step_install_power_watch_unit:936-991 and step_install_boot_progress_units), but the $changed gate is set TRUE only when the UNIT FILE differs (cmp -s); when only Python source changes -- the common code-deploy case -- $changed=false and restart is silently skipped. Same anti-pattern in step_install_boot_progress_units. Fix: decouple service restart from unit-file-change gate; ensure eclipse-powerwatch + eclipse-obd restart on every code-or-unit deploy + audit all $changed-gated restarts + add PID-start-time verification post-restart pre-.deploy-version-bump. Pattern class: condition-gating defect (narrower predicate silently absorbing a broader case) -- same tokenization as F-7's edge-only post-grace check. Non-chain-blocking once worked around but every Pi deploy ships dead code until manual reboot.

Add PID-start-time verification post-restart, pre-.deploy-version-bump, that both services show STARTED later than deploy start. stories.intent: V0.27.17 deploy revealed two regressions Sprint 41 missed. I-041 (CRITICAL): US-351 added drive_statistics.data_quality column to SQLAlchemy model (src/server/db/models.py:711) but shipped no v0009 migration -- production MariaDB INSERT fails 100% with Unknown column data_quality. US-352 backfill 0/10. Third shape of false-pass class (V0.27.7 trigger-seam mock + V0.27.16 redo + V0.27.17 schema-vs-ORM mask via Base.metadata.create_all). US-355 deploy-context drive simulator did NOT catch it because the harness uses the same create_all masking pattern (own structural blind spot). I-042 (High): deploy-server.sh Step 4.9 wrote the idempotency marker despite 0/10 backfill success -- outcome-not-observed wrapper anti-pattern (same class as the V0.27.7/V0.27.16 trigger-seam false-pass cluster, this time at the deploy-script wrapper layer). Bundle both fixes into V0.27.18 patch loop on same sprint/sprint41-bugfixes-V0.27.17 branch. Bonus (NOT V0.27.18-blocking): US-355 harness invariant refinement -- apply migrations registry-only on fresh DB; do NOT call Base.metadata.create_all on the server engine; exposes schema-vs-ORM masking class structurally so the next column-add gap trips RED in CI not in production.
